Defendant Adam Barnum Dba Barnum Building & Design'S Motion For An Order Compelling Binding Arbitration
Off Calendar Jan-26-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christopher Cole, Esq. 415-279-9897 for Plaintiff Tracy Anne Sena.
Andre Hill, Esq. 415-397-9006 for Defendant Adam Barnum Dba Barnum Building & Design.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant informs the Court he agrees with the mandates of the tentatvie ruling and consolidation but has problems with the conduct of Plaintiff's Counsel Mr. Hurabiell. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentatve ruling as follows:

Defendant Adam Barnum Dba Barnum Building & Design's Motion For An Order Compelling Binding Arbitration.

Defendant Barnum's motion to compel arbitration is denied. The court determines that plaintiff fails to show unconscionability or duress under CACI 332 or 333 to warrant denial of arbitration. Even if defendant violated Business and Professions Code sec. 7159.5(a)(3), that illegal conduct is collateral to the main purpose of the contract and courts will generally sever an offensive clause and enforce the contract. (See Little v. Auto Stiegler, Inc. (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1064, 1074-1075.) Moreover, the claim of illegality is attenuated because the illegality is unrelated to the agreement to arbitrate. Courts view agreements to arbitrate as severable from the underlying contract. (See Bruni v. Didion (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 1272, 1283-1286.) Nevertheless, the court exercises its discretion under CCP 1281.2(c) and denies arbitration to prevent inconsistent rulings. This action and the subrogation action (CGC-17-559877) share common issues of law and fact. The parties are ordered to meet and confer with CSAA regarding consolidating the two actions. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/RU)
